title: Portfolio and the Project Status Summary Widgets
excerpt:
Description
Currently, the widgets for chart and list are showing as non exportable when added to a Project Status Summary.  Can we ensure all widgets are exportable so they reflect appropriately on a PDF and not just a …

posts.author: Roctor1 (Rockzann Torgerson )
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Currently, the widgets for chart and list are showing as non exportable when added to a Project Status Summary. Can we ensure all widgets are exportable so they reflect appropriately on a PDF and not just a word document?

What are you trying to achieve

I am trying to create clean reports to give to clients by streamlining what is shown on the Project Status Summary within the Portfolio

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yeah, this is one of the biggest limitations of the Project Status Summary - charts and list widgets don’t export properly, which totally breaks clean client reports.

If you need reliable PDF exports where all widgets render correctly, you might want to try Time in Status for monday.com. It lets you build time-based charts and lists and export everything into a clean PDF/Excel file without the formatting issues that Portfolio reports run into.

Super handy when you’re preparing polished client-facing summaries and can’t rely on the built-in export behavior.
